works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 1 tossups and 6 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: While living on this river, John Cozad killed a man in a land dispute, leading him to flee, change his name, and father the artist Robert Henri .
examples: While living on this river, John Cozad killed a man in a land dispute, leading him to flee, change his name, and father the artist Robert Henri .

clues.clue: The STAR WARS committee sanctions private residential communities on artificial lakes that dominate many cities on this river, including Valley and North Bend.
examples: The STAR WARS committee sanctions private residential communities on artificial lakes that dominate many cities on this river, including Valley and North Bend.

clues.clue: The west side of a city named for a tributary of this river is home to the largest railroad yard in the world, which is named for former Union Pacific president (*) Edd Bailey.
examples: The west side of a city named for a tributary of this river is home to the largest railroad yard in the world, which is named for former Union Pacific president (*) Edd Bailey.

clues.clue: While running parallel to this river, I-80 is spanned by a faux-wood archway filled with historical dioramas.
examples: While running parallel to this river, I-80 is spanned by a faux-wood archway filled with historical dioramas.

clues.clue: This river flows to the south of Columbus near its confluence with the Loup River.
examples: This river flows to the south of Columbus near its confluence with the Loup River.

clues.clue: This river was variously described as "too thick to drink, too thin to plow" and "a mile wide and an inch deep" by pioneers going west.
examples: This river was variously described as "too thick to drink, too thin to plow" and "a mile wide and an inch deep" by pioneers going west.
